steps.instruction: Fill a large pot with plenty of water (about 4-6 quarts per pound of pasta). Bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Add salt to the boiling water. A good rule of thumb is 1-2 tablespoons of salt per gallon of water. This seasons the pasta from the inside out.
Add your pasta to the boiling water. Stir immediately to prevent sticking.

Cook the pasta according to package directions, but start checking for doneness 1-2 minutes before the lower end of the suggested time. We're looking for 'al dente' - tender but still firm to the bite.
steps.check: Taste a piece of pasta. Is it tender but still has a slight firmness in the center?

steps.instruction: Before draining, reserve about 1 cup of the starchy pasta water. This will be useful for sauces.
Drain the pasta in a colander. Do not rinse it, as this removes the starch that helps sauce cling to it.
